title: Engineering Workstation Anomaly
app: EDR on EWS, Windows Security / Sysmon
spl:
index=ot sourcetype="sysmon:windows" host_tag="EWS"
| search EventCode=1 OR EventCode=11
| where NOT match(Image,"(?i)(approved\\\\path)")
| stats count by Computer, Image, ParentImage
| sort -count
description: USB inserts, new binaries, or remote sessions on EWS workstations are high-risk in OT.
value: USB inserts, new binaries, or remote sessions on EWS workstations are high-risk in OT.
implementation: Lock down EWS to approved paths. Alert on new process or driver load. Correlate with maintenance windows.
knownFalsePositives: Matches during patch cycles, GPO refreshes, approved remote vendor sessions, or image updates on engineering hosts.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: EDR on EWS, Windows Security / Sysmon.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="sysmon:windows"` `tag=ews`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Lock down EWS to approved paths. Alert on new process or driver load. Correlate with maintenance windows.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot sourcetype="sysmon:windows" host_tag="EWS"
| search EventCode=1 OR EventCode=11
| where NOT match(Image,"(?i)(approved\\\\path)")
| stats count by Computer, Image, ParentImage
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Engineering Workstation Anomaly** — USB inserts, new binaries, or remote sessions on EWS workstations are high-risk in OT.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="sysmon:windows"` `tag=ews`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): EDR on EWS, Windows Security / Sysmon. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot; **sourcetype**: sysmon:windows. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot, sourcetype="sysmon:windows". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• Filters the current rows with `where NOT match(Image,"(?i)(approved\\\\path)")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by Computer, Image, ParentImage** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare process, driver, and USB-related events to the standard image for the engineering workstation, the EDR or Windows security console, and your change or maintenance ticket. Confirm host naming and tag filters before escalating.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (suspicious processes), Timeline (EWS events).
grandmaExplanation: We watch your plant control systems and field data. We help you know if something is off with the engineering laptop or desktop in the control zone before harm, damage, or wasted effort piles up.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable (no CIM or Operational_Telemetry data model in this SPL).
